description: High-posterior anemia with severe low hemoglobin should trigger more explicit prompt medical follow-up language without sounding alarmist.
expectations: Patient-facing narrative clearly recommends prompt medical review
Anemia is not downplayed as routine

description: Prediabetes starts elevated in raw ML but drops materially after gating or Bayesian review, so the narrative should soften and de-prioritize it.
expectations: Narrative does not frame prediabetes as a leading concern
Language uses soft uncertainty and de-emphasis
No near-term or urgent CTA is attached to this condition

description: A medium-confidence kidney signal with reduced eGFR should produce near-term follow-up language, stronger than routine but less forceful than urgent.
expectations: Language is supportive and not panicked
Condition remains present in next-step prioritization

description: Low-confidence thyroid signal with weak cluster support should stay clearly tentative.
expectations: Narrative uses tentative wording such as may suggest or worth ruling out
No urgent or near-term escalation appears
Condition is not presented as established
